title: Phenomenon 45
wikitext:
{{Phenomenon Dreyfus Scale|threat=4|frequency=2|frequencydesc=False Keys only appear rarely and oftentimes in high stress environments|hazard=5|hazarddesc=These Keys have only ever sent wanderers to dangerous levels|enigma=3|enigmadesc=The danger is known but how the backrooms recognizes such levels is not}} '''Phenomenon 45''' also known as '''False Level Keys''' are a Phenomenon where a level key is disguised as a Safe [[Object 2|Level Key]] but really sends the wanderer to an unknown dangerous level. While this happens most often with [[Red Rooms|The Red Rooms]] where its Level Keys can take the appearance of different level keys, its known to affect a wide variety of levels. This Phenomenon is poorly understood, but it is known that a fake key feels flaky and can have its paint removed easily, which will reveal the illusion. == Discovery: == This Phenomenon has very likely affected the Backrooms for hundreds if not thousands of years. The first recorded example of this by modern sources was done in the 1800s when a [[M.E.G.]] expedition in [[Level 9]] ran into [[Entity 196|The Neighbor Watch]] and were forced to use a Level Key that they had found in a house. According to survivor testimony, this was a [[Object 2|Level Key]] for [[Level 22]] that for an unknown reason lead to [[Level 48]]. While some of the expedition were able to return back to safety, about two thirds of the M.E.G. agents are known to have died or disappeared along the way back to [[Level 1]]. With the survivors back home, it now fell on M.E.G. to figure out what happened with the expedition. As [[Level 22]] was safe for human habitation at this time as the [[Object 3|Nuclear Infection]] and Level Degradation would not happen for another hundred or so years, it was therefore considered weird that a [[Object 2|Level Key]] that was for a safe level would send them to a then-unknown dangerous level. As more and more reports came back to M.E.G. Command about these '''False Keys''', another expedition was eventually able to be take one back to [[Level 1]] to be studied. == Known Properties: == Once the '''False Key''' was back in [[Level 1]], a handful of [[M.E.G.]] agents tried to figure out what was off about these, and were eventually able to get enough of the paint that disguised the level keys true appearance off. In this case, a Key that looked like one that was leading to [[Level 4]] would instead lead people to [[Level 6]]. This proved that it was possible for the Backrooms to be able to disguise level keys and this was officially listed as a Phenomenon, with it being one of the first recorded by MEG officials instead of a predecessor organization. It has been noted to disguise itself as keys for Safe Backrooms Levels and will always lead to Dangerous Backrooms levels. In one grueling case in the 1990s, a wanderer is known to have found a '''False Key''' to [[Level 799]] that was disguised as a [[Level 399]] key. It is not fully known what happened to them, however it is most likely that they ended up meeting their end at Johnny's hands as the level was still new then. It is also not known how this Phenomenon is able to know how a level is dangerous or not. Before they underwent [[Object 3|Nuclear Infection]], this never sent wanderers to either [[Level 22]] or [[Level 630]], however the Phenomenon now does. In addition a number of levels that have gone from being dangerous to being safe have stopped being sent wanderers this way. It seems that this Phenomenon has at least some level of sentience over what backrooms levels are dangerous for humans to be in. == Known Exit Levels: == For the sake of making the danger the '''False Level Keys''' provide to wanderers known, [[M.E.G.]] has compiled a list of some of the known levels that '''False Keys''' have sent wanderers to. This list is not comprehensive and will be updated as new information gets added to the database === Currently Known False Level Key Exits: === * [[Red Rooms]] * [[Level 116]] * [[Level 237]] * [[Level 350]] * [[Level 442]] * [[Level 483.5]] * [[Level 598]] * [[Level 777]] == Surviving False Level Keys: == While a vast majority of wanderers will never encounter a '''False Key'''Indeed most will never even encounter a normal level key, this does not mean you can rest your guard about them. These keys have been known to appear at the worst possible timesMost often when dangerous entities are nearby, and can even catch wanderers who know better off guard. Therefore, [[M.E.G.]] feels as though it is better for there to be some basic knowledge for those to be able to quickly and easily figure out what a false level key is. === Tip 1: Do Not Trust Keys in Dangerous Levels === The first and most important tip to deal with these '''False Keys''' is that mistrust should be given to keys in dangerous levels. While not all (or even most) Safe [[Object 2|Level Keys]] on Dangerous levels are false, there's still enough that caution should be exercised. There have been far too many instances of people finding a "safe" key leading to [[Level 4]] while on [[Level 9]] only to end up in a Class Deadzone level instead. Do not doubt that any key you see could be an impostor waiting to strike at the worse moment. === Tip 2: Be Mindful of Alternative Escape Routes === The worst thing you can do in this situation is panic. If you end up in a Class Deadzone, you're probably already doomed. However most non Deadzone levels are survivable if you have the knowledge and resources on how to deal with them. In very rare cases, '''False Keys''' have ironically sent people to safer levels than they started out on. While this should never be relied on as an actual tactic to survive, this has happened before and will happen again. === Tip 3: Keep the Keys with you === While many might think about keeping them behind instead due to the danger they pose, there are actually three major reasons as to why you should take a '''False Key''' with you. The first and more obvious is that you should take it so that someone who doesn't know about '''False Keys''' doesn't take them without the proper knowledge. It is best for everyone that you collect a '''False Key''' and keep it until it can be disposed of or given to proper authorities. The second reason is that if you are being chased and it looks like an entity may catch up, it might be worth it to switch levels in order to give yourself a better shot at survival. A small chance of survival is better than no chance of survival. Last off, even a '''False Key''' is considered valuable by many in the Backrooms as it can be used to detect dangerous level entrances alongside being rare defects of an already rare object. == Footnotes == * __NOTOC__ {{Collapse|initial='''> While Falsehoods might try to deceive us...'''|final='''> ...It is Truth that wins in the end'''|body='''Author''': [[User:ClassicLim|ClassicLim]]
its good to be back from my 5.5 year hiatus}} [[Category:Phenomena]] [[Category:Dangerous]]
